## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(Î, E_Î)`** â finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal â not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, âĪ^d, âĶ) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the MarshallâLiebâMattis theorem, Miyao 2021
Â§3, âĶ) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `Î : Type*` together with `[Fintype Î]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `âĪ` or `âĪ^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : Î â Î â â` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and CollatzâWielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank â âĪ 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D âĨ 0` version because single-ion `Âą2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D âĨ 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **Îŧ=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|Î|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `Îŧ = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `Îŧ = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 âĪ N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `Îŧ = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `Îŧ âĨ 1`, `D âĪ 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M âĐ Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M âĐ Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t â Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the Âą2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 âĪ N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < Îŧ < 1`, `D âĨ 0`; `Îŧ = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < Îŧ`, `D âĪ 0`. |

`EigenvalueMonotone.lean`). **Corollary A.8** (`ÃâĪBĖ â Tr e^Ã âĪ Tr e^BĖ`, eq A.2.31) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`MatrixAnalysis/TraceExpMonotone.lean`: spectral mapping `Tr e^Ã = ÎĢ e^{a_j}` via `Matrix.exp_conj` + `exp_diagonal` + `trace_mul_cycle`, then A.7 Weyl + `Real.exp` monotone). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `ÄĪ=ÎĢÄĨâąž`, `ÄĨâąžâĨÎĩâąž`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate â ground state at `ÎĢÎĩâąž`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`ÃâĨ0 â§ âĻÎĶ|Ã|ÎĶâĐ=0 â ÃÎĶ=0`; `Ã=BĖâ BĖ â BĖÎĶ=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `ÄīÂēÎĶ=0 â Äīâ―áĩâūÎĶ=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidef/Kernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`ÄĪ_v=ÄĪâ+vVĖ`, `VĖâĨ0`: `vââ` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `ÎĶ` satisfy the effective SchrÃķdinger eq `PĖâÄĪâÎĶ=EÎĶ` on `Hâ=ker VĖ`) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto: the paired eigenvalue relation gives `vÂ·âĻÎĶ_v|VĖ|ÎĶ_vâĐ = E_vâÎĶ_vâÂēââĻÎĶ_v|ÄĪâ|ÎĶ_vâĐ`, the converging right side times `vâŧÂđâ0` kills the limiting quadratic form, Lemma A.11 kills `VĖÎĶ`; pairing with `Ï â ker VĖ` removes the `vVĖ` term outright and the limit is the weak equation). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **Â§A.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) â operator identity `ÄīâŧÄīâš=ÄīÂēâÄīÂģ(ÄīÂģ+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`âJâĪM<J`, `ÎĶâ 0` â `ÄīâšÎĶâ 0`) + raising **membership** (`ÄīâšÎĶâH_{J,M+1}`, via `[ÄīÂģ,Äīâš]=Äīâš` + `[ÄīÂē,Äīâš]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`ÄīâšÄīâŧ=ÄīÂēâÄīÂģ(ÄīÂģâ1)` eq A.3.8, `ÄīâŧÎĶâ 0` for `âJ<MâĪJ`, `ÄīâŧÎĶâH_{J,Mâ1}`) **proved** â A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Ladder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `âÄīÂąÎĶâÂē={J(J+1)âM(MÂą1)}âÎĶâÂē` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian Äī^Îą) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`âJâĪMâĪJ`, i.e. `JâM,J+MâĨ0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `Äīâš`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `JâM â âĪâĨ0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(ÄīÂđ,âÄīÂē,âÄīÂģ)` gives `J+M â âĪâĨ0`, so `2J=(JâM)+(J+M)ââĪâĨ0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Quantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `ÄĪ` (`[ÄĪ,Äīáĩ]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,Jâk}` (`kâĪ2J`) â `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is âĨ`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Multiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `ÄĪ` has an eigenstate with `ÄīÂģ=0` or `ÄīÂģ=1/2` â `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is **now proved (axiom-free)** via the generic common eigenvector of two commuting Hermitian operators on an invariant subspace (`Math/CommutingHermitianEigenvector.lean`: `exists_common_eigenvector_of_isHermitian_commute`), the Casimir `ÄīÂē` being PSD and commuting with `ÄīÂģ`/`ÄĪ`, `AngularMomentum/SpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (PerronâFrobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(cÂ·1âM)` irreducible (off-diag `âĪ0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector â `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's CollatzâWielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D Vâ `, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d âĨ 0`) â **now proved (axiom-free)**: SVD built from the spectral theorem of `AáīīA` (`eigenvectorUnitary` `V`, eigenvalues `Îŧ_i âĨ 0`, `d_i = âÎŧ_i`), with the normalised images `u_i = d_iâŧÂđÂ·A v_i` extended to an orthonormal basis (`exists_orthonormalBasis_extension_of_card_eq`) giving `U`, and `A V = U D` columnwise; polar follows as `W = U Váīī`, `C = V D Váīī` (`MatrixAnalysis/Decomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear â-automorphism of `M(H)` âđ `Î(Ã)=Ãâ ÃÃ`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear âđ `Ãâ ÃĖÃ`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P Pâē]` âđ (anti)unitary `VĖ`) â documented axioms over `Matrix D D â` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / BanachâAlaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-â-continuous `Ï : WeakDual â A` with `Ï 1 = 1`, `0 âĪ Ï(aâ a)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-â compact, documented axiom) â `CStarAlgebra/State.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `Îī=[ÄĪ,Â·]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 âĪ Ï(aâ Â·Îīa)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`Îģ>0`, `Ï(aâ Îīa) âĨ ÎģÏ(aâ a)` on `Ï(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state âš `Ï(ÄĪ_L)` least over states agreeing outside Î_L, documented axiom) â `CStarAlgebra/GroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` â every state `Ï` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_Ï, Ï_Ï, ÎĐ_Ï)` (`â`-representation `Ï : A âââ[â] (HâL H)` + cyclic `ÎĐ`) with `Ï(Ã)=âĻÎĐ,Ï(Ã)ÎĐâĐ` and `{Ï(Ã)ÎĐ}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarAlgebra/GNS.lean`. **Â§A.6âÂ§A.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete â Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1âA.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (Â§11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** â the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.1** (the Lie product / Trotter formula, proved
  from scratch in a complete normed `â`-algebra), **A.12** (the strong-coupling
  effective Hamiltonian limit, by the `vâŧÂđ`-squeeze + kernel-pairing continuity
  argument), **A.4âA.6**
  (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.7âA.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity via CourantâFischer,
  and the trace-exp monotonicity through the spectral mapping for `exp`),
  **A.9âA.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13âA.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), **A.17** (spin-0/half sector via the common
  eigenvector of commuting Hermitians), **A.18** (PerronâFrobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's CollatzâWielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument), and **A.19âA.20** (polar + singular-value
  decompositions from the spectral theorem).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** â the
  operator-algebraic **A.21âA.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, BanachâAlaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* â they record exactly what Tasaki proves â but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. They fall into two categories:

- **Operator-algebraic results** (Appendix A.21âA.28): the heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic structures (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-â
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**.

- **Perturbation-theoretic results** (e.g., **Lemma 10.1** (Tasaki Â§10.1, degenerate
perturbation theory) and singular-perturbation arguments in Chapter 10): the analytic
proofs of weak-coupling continuation and adiabatic following for eigenstate families
are **not undertaken** as an active project goal; such techniques naturally belong to
a separate analytic-perturbation development. Results whose only missing link is a
perturbation-theoretic proof â such as the total-spin values in Lieb's repulsive-Hubbard
theorem (Theorem 10.4) â are recorded as documented axioms while their companion
finite-coupling combinatorial bounds are proved axiom-free.

- **Book theorems that Tasaki states without proof** (results he quotes from the
external literature rather than proving in the text): **Theorem 10.11** (KuboâKishi
finite-temperature charge/pairing susceptibility bound, Tasaki Â§10.2.5, citing
KuboâKishi, *Phys. Rev. B* **41**, 4866 (1990)) and **Theorem 11.13** (Mielke's
flat-band ferromagnetism, `mielke_theorem_11_13`) are recorded as **faithful documented
axioms** on the concrete finite-volume operators (here the Duhamel susceptibilities),
matching the "Tasaki states it without proof" policy â the reproving of the cited
external work is not an active project goal.

Accordingly the project's policy is to **axiomatize only the appendix and
perturbation-theory results that Tasaki's formalized main development actually uses**,
to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the tools, and otherwise to
leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in large bespoke developments whose
natural home is elsewhere. The `#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes
the precise dependency on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Pauli-basis decomposition (Tasaki Â§2.1 Problem 2.1.a, S = 1/2)

|---|---|---|

| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `Å^(1) := (Å^+ + Å^-) / 2`, `Å^(2) := (Å^+ â Å^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `Å^+` annihilates `\|NâĐ` (highest weight); `Å^-` annihilates `\|0âĐ` (lowest weight) â the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[Å^{(3)}, Å^+] = Å^+` and `[Å^{(3)}, Å^-] = -Å^-`: `Å^Âą` shift the magnetic quantum number by `Âą1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (Îē-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(Å^+)áīī = Å^-`, `(Å^-)áīī = Å^+`, and Hermiticity of `Å^{(Îą)}` for `Îą â {1, 2, 3}` (Îē-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `Å^{(1)}` and `Å^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(Îī_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(Å^{(3)} â Îŧ_j âĒ 1) Â· P_k = (Îŧ_k â Îŧ_j) Â· P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (Îē-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `â_{j â  k} (Å^{(3)} â Îŧ_j âĒ 1) / (Îŧ_k â Îŧ_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `Å^{(3)} Â· P_k = Îŧ_k Â· P_k` (where `Îŧ_k = (N : â)/2 â k`), with the symmetric `P_k Â· Å^{(3)} = Îŧ_k Â· P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (Îē-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A Â· P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k Â· A`). Specialised to `Å^Âą` produces off-diagonal matrix units (Îē-6 of Issue #458) â the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `Å^+ Â· P_{k+1} Â· Å^- = (k + 1)(N â k) Â· P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `Å^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1Ė, Å^{(Îą)}}` (Îē-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `Å^+ Â· P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `Å^- Â· P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `Å^Âą` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (Îē-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `ÎĢ_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (Îē-6, Îē-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(â)` (Îē-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `Å^+ Â· P_{i+1} = â((i+1)(Nâi)) Â· E_{i, i+1}` and `Å^- Â· P_i = â((Nâi)(i+1)) Â· E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (Îē-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `Å^Âą` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(Å^{(3)})Âē = diag((N/2 â i)Âē)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(Å^{(1)})Âē + (Å^{(2)})Âē + (Å^{(3)})Âē = (N(N+2)/4) Â· 1` for general spin (Îē-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `Å^+ Â· Å^- = diag((i + 1)(N â i))`. The product is diagonal because `Å^+[i, l] Â· Å^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `Å^- Â· Å^+` and `(Å^{(3)})Âē`, this assembles the Casimir identity (Îē-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `Å^- Â· Å^+ = diag(i Â· (N â i + 1))`. Symmetric to Îē-12 with `Å^Âą` swapped (Îē-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(Å^{(1)})Âē + (Å^{(2)})Âē + (Å^{(3)})Âē = (N(N+2)/4) Â· 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) Â· 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(Å^{(1)})Âē + (Å^{(2)})Âē = (1/2)(Å^+ Â· Å^- + Å^- Â· Å^+)` is proved using `module` (Îē-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `Å^{(3)} Â· \|kâĐ = (N/2 â k) Â· \|kâĐ`, where `\|kâĐ := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) â â)` (Îē-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `Å^+ Â· \|kâĐ = â(k(N â k + 1)) Â· \|k â 1âĐ` for `k âĨ 1`, and `Å^- Â· \|kâĐ = â((N â k)(k + 1)) Â· \|k + 1âĐ` for `k âĪ N â 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (Îē-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(Å)Âē Â· \|kâĐ = (N(N+2)/4) Â· \|kâĐ`. Direct consequence of `(Å)Âē = (N(N+2)/4) Â· 1` (Îē-14) â every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (Îē-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `Å^{(Îą)}` and `Å^Âą` commutes with the Casimir `(Å)Âē = (N(N+2)/4) Â· 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (Îē-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[Å^+, Å^-] = 2 Â· Å^{(3)}`. Combines Îē-12 (`Å^+ Â· Å^- = diag((i + 1)(N â i))`) and Îē-13 (`Å^- Â· Å^+ = diag(i (N â i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) â i(N-i+1) = N â 2i = 2(N/2 â i)` matches `2 Â· Å^{(3)}` (Îē-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[Å^{(1)}, Å^{(2)}] = i Â· Å^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `Å^{(1)} = (Å^+ + Å^-)/2` and `Å^{(2)} = (Å^+ â Å^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) â (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 Å^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (Îē-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[Å^{(2)}, Å^{(3)}] = i Â· Å^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan Îē-2 (`[Å^{(3)}, Å^Âą] = ÂąÅ^Âą`) via `Å^{(2)} = (Å^+ â Å^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-Å^+ â Å^-) = (-1/(2i)) Â· 2 Å^{(1)} = i Â· Å^{(1)}` (Îē-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[Å^{(3)}, Å^{(1)}] = i Â· Å^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan Îē-2 via `Å^{(1)} = (Å^+ + Å^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (Å^+ â Å^-) = (1/2) Â· (2i) Â· Å^{(2)} = i Â· Å^{(2)}`. Together with Îē-20 and Îē-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (Îē-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |kâĐâĻk|`: `P_k Â· P_k = P_k` and `P_i Â· P_j = 0` for `i â  j`. Combined with Îē-9 (`â_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `Å^{(3)}` (Îē-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. | `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (Å^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = â_{j â  k} (Å^{(3)} â Îŧ_j âĒ 1)/(Îŧ_k â Îŧ_j)` with `Îŧ_j = (N : â)/2 â j.val`. Combines Îē-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. | `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `Å^+ = Å^{(1)} + i Â· Å^{(2)}` and `Å^- = Å^{(1)} â i Â· Å^{(2)}`. | `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k â Algebra.adjoin â {Å^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k â Algebra.adjoin â {Å^{(1)}, Å^{(2)}, Å^{(3)}}`). | `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin â {Å^{(Îą)}}`**: `Å^+, Å^- â Algebra.adjoin â {Å^{(1)}, Å^{(2)}, Å^{(3)}}`. | `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin â {Å^{(Îą)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} â Algebra.adjoin â {Å^{(1)}, Å^{(2)}, Å^{(3)}}`. Combines Îē-10 (`Å^+ Â· P_{i+1} = â((i+1)(N-i)) Â· E_{i, i+1}`) with Îē-27 (`P_k â adjoin`) and Îē-28 (`Å^Âą â adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. | `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin â {Å^{(Îą)}}`**. | `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **ðŊ Tasaki Â§2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `â^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1Ė, Å^{(1)}, Å^{(2)}, Å^{(3)}}`. ### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, Â§2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `Î` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `Î = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp Î` | `Matrix (Î â Fin 2) (Î â Fin 2) â` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : Î` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian â (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x â  y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A Â· onSite i B = onSite i (A Â· B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian (generic; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4342) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |

### Generic matrix-analysis helpers (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/`)

Topic-organized generic linear-algebra facts extracted from the physics files where they had been
re-proved as private helpers (Issue [#4339](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4339)).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` | a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | the product of two commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_isHermitian` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_sq_of_sq_one` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting involutions is an involution | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mul_self_of_idempotent` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting idempotents is idempotent | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mulVec_eq_self` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of matrices each fixing `Ï` also fixes `Ï` | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | the trace of a Hermitian matrix is real | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `Tr(AÂ·B)` of two Hermitian matrices is conjugation-invariant | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |

(The two trace helpers above are also listed in the Gibbs-state section where they are consumed; the
canonical definitions live in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean`.)

These are consumed by the JordanâWigner string / Hubbard hard-core projection layers (PR #4342),
replacing the per-file private copies in `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean`,
`Fermion/JordanWigner/Operators.lean`, and `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean`.

### Horschâvon der Linden low-lying states (Tasaki Â§3.4, Theorem 3.1)

Start of the Chapters 3â10 backfill (Issue
[#4485](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4485); book order, infinite systems in
scope). Tasaki Â§3.4, Theorem 3.1, eqs. (3.4.7)â(3.4.12), pp. 66â67.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `horsch_vonderLinden_lowLying` | for Hermitian `H` with min eigenvalue `Eâ = eigenvalues iâ`, a unit state `Î` orthogonal to the ground eigenvector with `âĻÎ,HÎâĐ âĪ Eâ+Îī` yields an energy eigenstate `j â  iâ` with `Eâ âĪ E_j âĪ Eâ+Îī` (a low-lying state; possibly another ground state if degenerate, as Tasaki notes) | `Quantum/HorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `kaplan_horsch_vonderLinden_order_lower_bound` | **Theorem 3.2, finite-volume core** (Â§3.4; the thermodynamic double limit 3.4.22 is not formalized): for the field-perturbed ground state `ÎĻ` of `H â hÂ·O` (`h>0`) and any trial `Î`, the order parameter obeys `âĻÎ,OÎâĐ + (EâââĻÎ,HÎâĐ)/h âĪ âĻÎĻ,OÎĻâĐ` (eq. (3.4.21), the variational core; the double limit `Lââ,hâ0` gives the âqâ SSB bound) | `Quantum/KaplanHorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS` / `staggeredOrderOpS_isHermitian` | **Theorem 4.1** (Â§4.1, DysonâLiebâSimon, toward; eq. (4.1.7)): the staggered (NÃĐel) order operator `Ã = ÎĢ_x Îĩ_x Å_x^(3)` (`Îĩ_x = Âą1` sublattice sign) and its Hermiticity (real Âą1 combination of Hermitian `Å_x^(3)`), so `âĻÎĶ,ÃÂēÎĶâĐ` is a real order parameter. The NÃĐel-LRO statement needs the actual hypercubic torus + reflection positivity (false for arbitrary bipartite graphs); deferred pending that infrastructure (infinite-volume in scope) | `Quantum/SpinS/DysonLiebSimon.lean` |
| `shastry_no_symmetry_breaking_1d` | **Theorem 4.2** (Â§4.1, Shastry, AXIOM; eqs. (4.1.9)â(4.1.10)): absence of SSB in 1D. For the 1D AFM Heisenberg ring under a staggered field `ÄĪ_h = ÎĢ_x Å_xÂ·Å_{x+1} â hÂ·Ã_L^(3)`, the per-site staggered order parameter of any normalized ground state vanishes in the iterated limit `lim_{hâ0} lim_{Lââ}` (eq. (4.1.10), ÎĩâÎī form). Tasaki states it without proof; the deep argument is now being formalized as the **reflection-positivity infrastructure project** (#4777, codex-validated 11-PR plan). Defines `ringCoupling`, `ringStaggeredSublattice`, `staggeredFieldChainHamiltonianS`. **RP infra layer 1 (in progress):** `RingBondReflection.lean` â even-ring bond reflection `ringReflect n x = 2nâ1âx` (involutive, half-swap, staggered-sign flip); `RingReflectionTheta.lean` â reflection map `Îļ(A) Ï Ï = conj(A(ÏÏ)(ÏÏ))`, an antilinear `*`-automorphism, with the single-site bridge `Îļ(onSiteS x A) = onSiteS (ringReflect n x) (conj A)`. **RP infra layer 2** (`RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`): the Hamiltonian Îļ-decomposition â orientation reversal `J(r x)(r y)=J y x`, spin-op conjugation (SÂđ/SÂģ real, SÂē imaginary), `Îļ(Å_xÂ·Å_y)=Å_{r x}Â·Å_{r y}`, `Îļ(Ã_L^(3))=âÃ_L^(3)` (staggered-sign flip), `Îļ(ÄĪ)=ÄĪ` (reindex + adjacency + dot-comm), and `Îļ(ÄĪ_h)=ÄĪ_{âh}` (the reflection symmetry exchanging the two staggered ground states). **RP infra layer 3** (`RingReflectionPositivity.lean`): the left-half subalgebra `SupportedOnLeftS` = `B(H_left) â I_right` (two conditions: entries vanish off the right-diagonal **and** are independent of the common right-half value) with closure (zero/one/add/smul, left-site `onSiteS`), `Îļ` maps left-supported to right-supported (`theta_right`), and the reflection-positive functional predicate `ReflectionPositiveFunctionalS` (`0 âĪ Re Ï(Îļ(A)Â·A)` for left-supported `A`). **RP infra layer 4** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`): the `Îē = 0` base case â `traceFunctional_reflectionPositive` proves the trace functional `X âĶ Tr X` is reflection positive, i.e. `0 âĪ Re Tr(Îļ(A)Â·A)` for every left-supported `A`. The proof collapses `Tr(Îļ(A)Â·A) = â_{Ï,Îž} Îļ(A)ÏÎžÂ·AÎžÏ` to its diagonal (both support conditions force `Îž=Ï`), then factorizes over the left/right configuration split (`configSplitEquiv`, from `finSumFinEquiv`): the diagonal value `A Ï Ï` depends only on the left half, giving `Tr(Îļ(A)Â·A) = conj S Â· S = âSâÂē âĨ 0` with `S = â_â D â`. This is the infinite-temperature trace cone on which a later layer mounts the Gibbs exponential via Trotter. **RP infra layer 5** (`RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`): the algebraic completion of the cone â `SupportedOnLeftS.mul` (the left-half subalgebra is closed under products), `SupportedOnLeftS.mul_theta_comm` (a left-supported `A` commutes with `Îļ(B)`, which acts on the right half), and the **weighted trace cone** `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive` (`X âĶ Tr((Îļ(C)Â·C)Â·X)` is reflection positive for left-supported `C`, via `Tr((ÎļCÂ·C)Â·(ÎļAÂ·A)) = Tr(Îļ(CÂ·A)Â·(CÂ·A)) âĨ 0`) plus its nonnegative-finite-combination version `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive_finsetSum` â the cone on which the Trotter/Lie-product factors of the Gibbs exponential will be mounted. **RP infra layer 6** (`RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`): the reflection-positive trace-weight cone â `RPTraceWeightS M` (`X âĶ Tr(MÂ·X)` is an RP functional), the cone-representability predicate `RPTraceConeRepS` (nonnegative finite combination `â cáĩĒ Îļ(CáĩĒ)Â·CáĩĒ`) with closure under `one`/`zero`/`add`/`smul_nonneg`/`mul`/`pow` (the product closure via `weightGen_mul`: `(ÎļCÂ·C)(ÎļDÂ·D) = Îļ(CD)Â·(CD)`), `RPTraceConeRepS.rpTraceWeight` (representable âđ RP trace weight, from the weighted cone), and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` (RP trace weights are closed under limits, by finite-dimensional trace continuity) â the cone on which the next layer mounts the interaction Gibbs exponential `exp(tÂ·âcáĩĒÎļ(CáĩĒ)Â·CáĩĒ)` as a limit of cone-representable partial sums. **RP infra layer 7** (`RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`): `rpInteractionExp_reflectionPositive` â for a nonnegative finite interaction `D = tÂ·âáĩĒ cáĩĒ Îļ(CáĩĒ)Â·CáĩĒ` (`t, cáĩĒ âĨ 0`, `CáĩĒ` left-supported), the matrix exponential `exp D` is a reflection-positive trace weight. Each partial sum `â_{k<m}(k!)âŧÂđDáĩ` of the exponential series is cone-representable (`smul_nonneg`/`add`/`pow`), hence an RP trace weight, and `exp D` is their limit (`expSeries_summable'.hasSum.tendsto_sum_nat`), so `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` applies. Uses the `Lâ`-operator-norm Banach structure (`open scoped Matrix.Norms.Operator`), whose topology is the entrywise (Pi) topology â the same one underlying the trace continuity, so no topology diamond. **RP infra layer 8** (`RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`): the matrix exponential and the left subalgebra / reflection map â `SupportedOnLeftS.exp` (`X` left-supported âđ `exp X` left-supported: the left subalgebra is closed under products/sums/scalars/entrywise-limits via `SupportedOnLeftS.of_tendsto`, and `exp X` is the limit of its partial sums) and `ringReflectionThetaS_exp` (`Îļ(exp X) = exp(Îļ X)`: `Îļ` is a continuous conjugate-linear `*`-automorphism with real exponential coefficients) â the building blocks for the `e^{-ÎēH_L}` Hamiltonian factor of the full Gibbs reflection-positivity decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/ShastryNoSSB.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondReflection.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTheta.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionPositivity.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `falkBruch_double_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkâBruch** (`FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the concrete ground-state FalkâBruch inequality â for a ground state `ÎĶ`, Hermitian `A` and a potential `y` for `AÎĶ` (`(HâEâ)y = AÎĶ`), `2(ReâĻÎĶ,AÂēÎĶâĐ)Âē âĪ ReâĻÎĶ,[A,[H,A]]ÎĶâĐÂ·ReâĻy,AÎĶâĐ`; combines the abstract FalkâBruch (`K = HâEâ` PSD) with the double-commutator = variational-energy identity | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean` |

| `ringTranslateConj` / `chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of` | **RP infra layer 39** (`RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the ring translation `Ï(X) = Tâ Â·XÂ·T` is a trace-preserving `*`-homomorphism (`ringTranslateConj_mul`/`_trace`), so any chessboard estimate transports unchanged to the translation-conjugated data `(ÏM, Ï(ÎļA), ÏB)` (`chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of`) â the weight `ÏM` is the Marshall gauge relative to the shifted reflection plane; carries the single-axis chessboard to every bond toward Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_gibbs` / `thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation` | **RP infra layer 38** (`RingTranslationGibbs.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): unlike the half-system gauge, the physical ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_heisenbergHamiltonianS`), so the physical Gibbs operator `exp(âÎēÂ·ÄĪ)` is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_gibbs`) and every physical thermal average is translation covariant `âĻTâ ATâĐ = âĻAâĐ` (`thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation`); the thermal-state translation symmetry for the momentum-space infrared analysis | `Quantum/SpinS/RingTranslationGibbs.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_ringReflectionThetaS_onSiteS` / `_spinSDot` | **RP infra layer 37** (`RingReflectionTranslation.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): translation covariance of the fixed-axis reflection map `Îļ` â conjugating `Îļ(onSiteS x A)` (resp. `Îļ(Å_xÂ·Å_y)`) by the ring translation `chainTranslationOp` shifts the reflection axis, sending the sites to `finRotate (ringReflect x)` (resp. both endpoints); the foundation for reflections across every bond, needed to spread the single-axis chessboard toward DLS Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTranslation.lean` |
| `ringDLSGibbs_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 36** (`RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the concrete chessboard inequality for the gauged ring Gibbs weight â combining its reflection positivity (Gibbs capstone) and reflection invariance (`ÎļM=M`), for `Îē âĨ 0` and left-supported `A,B`, `(Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļAÂ·B))Âē âĪ Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļAÂ·A)Â·Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļBÂ·B)` with `M = exp(âÎēÂ·toHamiltonian)`; the DLS factorization estimate for the thermal state of the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_toHamiltonian` / `_ringDLSGibbs` | **RP infra layer 35** (`RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauged DLS Hamiltonian is reflection-invariant `Îļ(toHamiltonian) = toHamiltonian` (the `H_L + Îļ(H_L)` part is swapped to itself; each crossing bond `Îļ(crossBondInteractionS x) = crossBondInteractionS x` since `Å_x^Îą` commutes with its right-half reflection `Îļ(Å_x^Îą)`), hence the gauged ring Gibbs weight `exp(âÎēÂ·toHamiltonian)` is reflection-invariant â supplying the `ÎļM = M` hypothesis for the chessboard/real-diagonal lemmas | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean` |
| `rpForm_re_symm` / `rpTraceWeight_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 34** (`RingReflectionChessboard.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` (`ÎļM=M`) and left-supported `A,B`, the off-diagonal form value is symmetric `Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļAÂ·B) = Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļBÂ·A)` (since `A` commutes with the right-half `ÎļB`); feeding this into the CauchyâSchwarz discriminant collapses the cross term, giving the chessboard bound `(Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļAÂ·B))Âē âĪ Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļAÂ·A)Â·Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļBÂ·B)` â the DLS factorization step underlying Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_trace` / `rpForm_diag_im_zero` | **RP infra layer 33** (`RingReflectionTraceReality.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection map conjugates the trace `Tr(ÎļX) = conj(Tr X)` (reindex the diagonal by the involutive site reflection), so for a reflection-invariant weight `M` (`ÎļM = M`) and left-supported `A` the RP-form diagonal `Tr(MÂ·ÎļAÂ·A)` is real (`ÎļÂē=id`, `*`-homomorphism, `A` commutes with `ÎļA`); together with the CauchyâSchwarz this makes `(A,B)âĶTr(MÂ·ÎļAÂ·B)` a genuine positive-semidefinite form with real diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceReality.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz` | **RP infra layer 32** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): a reflection-positive trace weight `M` makes `(A,B) âĶ Tr(MÂ·ÎļAÂ·B)` a positive-semidefinite sesquilinear form on the left subalgebra; expanding `0 âĪ Re Tr(MÂ·Îļ(AâtB)Â·(AâtB))` as a nonnegative real quadratic in `t` and taking its discriminant (`discrim_le_zero`) gives `(Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļAÂ·B) + Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļBÂ·A))Âē âĪ 4Â·Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļAÂ·A)Â·Re Tr(MÂ·ÎļBÂ·B)` â the workhorse for the DLS infrared bound | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean` |
| `thermalAverageReS_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 31** (`RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge is a similarity transform, so it leaves the partition function invariant (`thermalPartitionFnS_toHamiltonian_eq`) and transports thermal averages â the physical ring Heisenberg canonical average of `A` equals the reflection-positive DLS canonical average of `rightGauge Â· A Â· rightGaugeInv`; the bridge carrying RP bounds back to the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean` |
| `exp_neg_smul_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 30** (`RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the right-half gauge commutes with the matrix exponential (`Matrix.exp_units_conj`), so the physical ring Heisenberg Gibbs operator `exp(âÎē ÄĪ)` and the reflection-positive DLS Gibbs operator `exp(âÎē Â· ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian)` are unitarily equivalent â physical thermal averages become DLS thermal averages of gauge-transformed observables | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean` |

| `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left`/`_right` | **RP infra layer 26** (`RingReflectionBondConj.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge fixes every non-crossing bond. `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left` (left bond, both sites `< n`: untouched) and `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_right` (right bond, both sites gauged: the `Å^1`/`Å^3` sign flips cancel in the product, `Å^2` unchanged). | `ringReflectionThetaS_ringLeftHamiltonian` | **RP infra layer 19** (`RingReflectionRightBondSum.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflected left bond Hamiltonian is the right bond Hamiltonian. | `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_eq_bondSum` | **RP infra layer 17** (`RingReflectionBondSum.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian as a sum over nearest-neighbor bonds. | `ringLeftHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft` | **RP infra layer 16** (`RingReflectionLeftHamiltonian.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the left part `H_L` of the ring DLS decomposition is left-supported. `SupportedOnLeftS.sum` (left-supportedness closed under finite sums); `heisenbergHamiltonianS_supportedOnLeft` (a Heisenberg Hamiltonian whose coupling `J x y` vanishes unless both sites are in the left half is left-supported, being `â_{x,y} J x y Â· Å_xÂ·Å_y` with each surviving term left-supported); `ringLeftCoupling` (the ring nearest-neighbor coupling restricted to left-half sites); and `ringLeftHamiltonian = heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringLeftCoupling n) N`, the concrete `H_L` that feeds `ringCrossingRPDecomposition`. | `ring_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (ring instance) | **RP infra layer 15** (`RingReflectionRingInstance.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the DLS decomposition of the gauged ring antiferromagnet. The concrete left-bond `H_L` and the unitary equivalence to `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n))` are the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRingInstance.lean` |
| `RPDecomposition.gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (**Gibbs RP capstone**) | **RP infra layer 14** (`RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): **full Gibbs reflection positivity** â for a reflection-positive decomposition `H = H_L + Îļ(H_L) â D` (`RPDecomposition`) and `Îē âĨ 0`, the Gibbs weight `exp(âÎēÂ·H)` is a reflection-positive trace weight. The DysonâLiebâSimon Trotter assembly: `e^{âÎēH} = e^{A+B}` (`A = âÎēÂ·(H_L+ÎļH_L)`, `B = ÎēÂ·D`), via the Lie product formula `e^{A+B} = lim_m (e^{A/m}Â·e^{B/m})^m` (`LieProduct.lieProductFormula`); each factor is consumed by an accumulating RP trace weight â the kinetic factor `e^{A/m} = exp(Y+ÎļY)` is cone-representable (`coneRep_exp_add` + `mul_coneRep_right`) and the interaction factor `e^{B/m}` is the exp of a cone-representable operator (`mul_exp_coneRep_right`) â then induction over the power and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto`. Helpers: `realSmul_add_theta`, `RPDecomposition.interaction_coneRep`. This is the reflection positivity at the heart of the DLS / Shastry argument | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_exp_coneRep_right` | **RP infra layer 13** (`RingReflectionMulExpConeRep.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): an RP trace weight absorbs the exponential of a cone-representable operator. If `M` is RP and `P` is `RPTraceConeRepS`, then `M Â· exp P` is RP â the partial sums `â_{k<m}(k!)âŧÂđPáĩ` are cone-representable (`pow`/`smul_nonneg`/`add`), so each `MÂ·(partial sum)` is RP (`mul_coneRep_right`), and `MÂ·exp P` is their limit (continuity of left multiplication + `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto`). This consumes the interaction Gibbs factor `e^{ÎēD/m}` in the Trotter expansion | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionMulExpConeRep.lean` |
| `coneRep_exp_add` (kinetic factor) | **RP infra layer 12** (`RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the kinetic Gibbs factor is cone-representable. For left-supported `X`, `exp(X + Îļ X) = exp X Â· Îļ(exp X) = Îļ(exp X) Â· exp X` (`ringReflectionThetaS_exp_add_eq`, via `exp_add_of_commute` since `X` and `Îļ(X)` commute, `Îļ(exp X) = exp(Îļ X)`, and `mul_theta_comm`); the right-hand side is a single cone generator `Îļ(L)Â·L` (`L = exp X` left-supported), so `coneRep_exp_add` gives `RPTraceConeRepS (exp(X + Îļ X))`. This is the kinetic building block (`e^{âÎēHâ/m}`) the Trotter expansion of `e^{âÎēH}` consumes | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right` (cone closure) | **RP infra layer 11** (`RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection-positive trace weights form a convex cone â `RPTraceWeightS.zero`/`one` (the identity is RP, the `Îē=0` trace cone), `add`, `smul_nonneg`, `sum` (closed under finite sums) â and `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right`: an RP trace weight times a cone-representable operator (`RPTraceConeRepS`) is again RP (since `MÂ·âcáĩĒÎļ(CáĩĒ)CáĩĒ = âcáĩĒ(MÂ·Îļ(CáĩĒ)CáĩĒ)`, each RP by the cone-generator closure, summed). This is the algebraic engine that accumulates reflection positivity factor-by-factor in the Trotter expansion of `e^{âÎēH}` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_weightGen_right` / `RPTraceWeightS.weightGen_mul_left` | **RP infra layer 10** (`RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): closure of reflection-positive trace weights under cone generators `Îļ(L)Â·L` (`L` left-supported). The naive "conjugation" closure `M âĶ LÂ·MÂ·Îļ(L)` is **false** (the homomorphism order `Îļ(LA)=Îļ(L)Îļ(A)` clashes with the test operator `B=AL`, and left-supported operators do not commute); the correct closure multiplies by a cone generator: `MÂ·(Îļ(L)Â·L)` is RP (substitute the test operator `A âĶ LÂ·A`, using `LÂ·Îļ(A)=Îļ(A)Â·L` and `Îļ(L)Â·Îļ(A)=Îļ(LÂ·A)`) and `(Îļ(L)Â·L)Â·M` is RP (substitute `A âĶ AÂ·L`, via trace cyclicity). These are the kinetic-factor building blocks of the full DysonâLiebâSimon Gibbs reflection positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean` |
| `crossBondInteractionS` / `RPDecomposition` | **RP infra layer 9** (`RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean`, Tasaki Â§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the DLS decomposition data and the crossing-bond interaction. `crossBondInteractionS x = ÎĢ_Îą Å_x^ÎąÂ·Îļ(Å_x^Îą)` (left site `x`), with the **gauge identity** `crossBondInteractionS x = Å_x^1 Å_{r x}^1 â Å_x^2 Å_{r x}^2 + Å_x^3 Å_{r x}^3` (the lone `â` on the `Å^2` term is the sign flipped by the DLS/Marshall gauge â a Ï-rotation around axis 2 on the right half â making all three components contribute the same negative reflection-positive sign). Each `onSiteS x Å^Îą` (`x<n`) is left-supported; `crossBondInteractionS_exp_rpTraceWeight` shows `exp(tÂ·crossBondInteractionS x)` (`tâĨ0`) is a reflection-positive trace weight (via `mul_theta_comm` + the interaction-exponential cone). | `no_long_range_order_1d` | **Corollary 4.3** (Â§4.1, AXIOM; eq. (4.1.11)): absence of LRO in 1D. | `raiseLowerReachableS_of_connected` | **Connected-graph spin-config reachability** (Â§2.5, PROVED; Issue #4609, prereq for the connected MarshallâLiebâMattis extension / Thm 4.4 axiom): on a connected graph `G`, any two equal-magnetization configs are raise/lower reachable â `G.Connected â magSumS Ï = magSumS Ï' â RaiseLowerReachableS G Ï Ï'`. This is the ladder step that makes `(Ã_Î)Âē`'s expectation constant across the spin-`S_tot` multiplet; **PR7 (#4616)** iterates it to `(Åâŧ_tot)^k` (`su2_expectationRatioRe_ladder_iterate_invariant`, `Quantum/SpinS/SU2ExpectationLadderIterated.lean`), so every member of the lowering tower shares the centered ratio. (4.2.12)/(4.2.13)/(4.2.35)/(4.2.45)â(4.2.47), footnote 21): the Tanaka state order-parameter lower bounds at every finite volume. **(4.2.12)**: `âqâ âĪ âĻÎ| Ã_L^(1)/L^d |ÎâĐ` (axis-1 per-site staggered moment, eq. 4.2.12 `sqrt_q0_le_tanakaOrderMean1`); **(4.2.13)**: `qâ âĪ âĻÎ| (Ã_L^(1)/L^d)Âē |ÎâĐ` (squared moment, eq. 4.2.13 `q0_le_tanakaOrderSecond1`). **Proof mechanism**: log-convexity of even bare moments `B_k := â(Ã_L^(1))^k ÎĶâÂē` (CauchyâSchwarz, eq. 4.2.35) yields non-decreasing ratio `R_k := B_{k+1}/(B_k VÂē)` via `real_logConvex_cross`; base case `R_0 âĨ qâ` from charge selection `âĻ(Ã^(1))ÂēâĐ = âĻpĖâĐ VÂē/2` combined with proved long-range-order bridge `âĻpĖâĐ âĨ 2qâ âÎĶâÂē` (`phatMoment_succ_two_q0_le`); hence `R_M âĨ qâ` via `orderOp1_evenMoment_ratio_ge_q0`; the Î sandwich `tanakaSSBState_dotProduct_mulVec_re_eq` with parity structure (odd-moment diagonals vanish, squared operator commutes with parity so cross-terms vanish) yields both bounds. Infrastructure: Hermiticity `staggeredOrderOp1S_isHermitian`, unit normalization `tanakaSSBState_vecNormSqRe_eq_one`. | `xyHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_5_1_xy_odlro_half_filling` | **Theorem 5.1** (Â§5.1âÂ§5.2, AXIOM; eqs. (5.1.1)â(5.2.5)): **off-diagonal long-range order (ODLRO) of hard-core bosons at half filling**. The bosonic order operators `Ã_L^(1,2)` (eqs. 5.2.2/5.2.4) are the staggered XY-plane spin order operators. Half filling is essential; the ground state shows LRO **without** SSB (eq. 5.2.8). | `CoupledSite` / `coupledHamiltonian` / `coupledCrossCorrelation` / `tasaki_5_4_coupled_bec_ssb` | **Theorem 5.4** (Â§5.5, AXIOM; eqs. (5.5.1)â(5.5.6)): **symmetry breaking in coupled BEC**. The coupled two-species lattice `CoupledSite = HypercubicTorus Ã Bool` (`(x,false)=a`, `(x,true)=b`); total Hamiltonian `ÄĪ_tot^Îĩ = ÄĪ_a + ÄĪ_b + Îĩ ÄĪ_tunnel` (`coupledHamiltonian`, eq. 5.5.1): the two uncoupled boson copies `ÄĪ_a+ÄĪ_b = 2(ÄĪ_XY,a+ÄĪ_XY,b)` (the boson factor `ÄĪ=2ÄĪ_XY`, consistent with `xyChemicalPotentialHamiltonianS`, so `Îĩ` is the textbook tunneling strength; via `sameSpeciesNNCoupling`) + the tunneling term `tunnelHamiltonian Ï = âÎĢ_x (e^{iÏ} Å^+_a Å^â_b + e^{âiÏ} Å^â_a Å^+_b)` (eq. 5.5.3). Total `2N` particles = doubled half filling `Å_tot^(3)=0`. Assuming single-system ODLRO (`hODLRO`: the uncoupled XY ground states satisfy the Theorem 5.1 bound with `qâ`, tying `qâ` to genuine ODLRO), the unique coupled ground state `ÎĶ^Îĩ` develops a definite relative U(1) phase: â `mĖ âĨ â(2qâ)` with `lim_{Îĩâ0} lim_{Lââ} âĻÎĶ^Îĩ, ÃĒâ _{(x,a)}ÃĒ_{(x,b)} ÎĶ^ÎĩâĐ/âĻÎĶ^Îĩ,ÎĶ^ÎĩâĐ = mĖÂēe^{âiÏ}` (eq. 5.5.5; `coupledCrossCorrelation = Å^+_{(x,a)}Å^â_{(x,b)}`) **and** the conjugate `âĻÎĶ^Îĩ, ÃĒ_{(x,a)}ÃĒâ _{(x,b)} ÎĶ^ÎĩâĐ/âĻÂ·âĐ = mĖÂēe^{+iÏ}` (eq. 5.5.6; `coupledCrossCorrelationConj = Å^â_{(x,a)}Å^+_{(x,b)}`). Double limit in eventual-`Îĩ'` form (outer `Îĩâ0`, inner `Lââ`); GS family given (unique by MLM). The two condensates are coherently coupled (entangled). Proof: KomaâTasaki. **Completes Tasaki Chapter 5** | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |

after splitting the cyclic wrap). **Slide lifted to a subtype step** (`slidePM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf`) and the **annihilation move** added: `annihPM` (set adjacent `a, b` both to `0`), / `notInCyclicOpen_pred_left`. **Move-count behaviour** added: `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf` (annihilation as a subtype step), `slidePM_pmCount_card` (sliding preserves the `Âą` count), slide (`slidePM_hhafS_lt`, non-wrapping successor) and an annihilation (`annihPM_hhafS_lt`); `hhaf_single_step` produces, for any balanced (even-`pmCount`) non-canonical config, a single HAF ladder `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf_symm` for the reverse). **Design note**: the *full* HAF matrix is reducible â a single-`Âą` config (`pmCount = 1`, magnetization `Âą1`) is vacuously hidden-AFM but unreachable from `canonical` (the moves conserve magnetization), so irreducibility holds only within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector; this is the sector containing the antiferromagnetic ground state. `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf0_symm` (balanced-sector connectivity lifted to the subtype), `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable0` (subtype walkâpower), and `hhafShiftedMatrix0_isIrreducible` â diagonal handled by the strict shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`). **Balanced-sector unique ground state PROVEN**: `hhafDressedMatrix0` (the dressed block on `hhafConfig0`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_isSymm` (submatrix of a symmetric matrix), `hhafShiftedMatrix0_eq` (`hhafShiftedMatrix0 = cÂ·I â Mâ`, the identity restricting since `Subtype.val` is injective), and `hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_finrank_le_one` â applying `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` to the balanced block yields a strictly positive lowest eigenvector with a **one-dimensional** ground eigenspace: the unique ground state of the AFM chain *within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector* of `H_HAF`. **Marshall-gauge transfer DONE**: `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0` (undressed real balanced block), `hhafMarshallDiag0` (the `Âą1` Marshall sign diagonal, squaring to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_eq_conj` (`Mâ = ÎÂ·M_realâÂ·Î`), and `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0_ground_finrank_le_one` â transferring the **full PerronâFrobenius data** (nonzero lowest eigenvector `Îv`, ground minimality, and `finrank âĪ 1`) through the Marshall similarity (the generic `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, now generalised from `â` to any field), so the **undressed** real balanced block also has a one-dimensional ground eigenspace with an explicit minimal eigenvector. Remaining for the *global* ground-state uniqueness: the inter-sector energy ordering (balanced sector strictly lowest, a MarshallâLiebâMattis statement within `H_HAF`). **NÃĐel witness** added (toward that ordering): `hhafNeel` (the maximally-AFM configuration `+1` on even sites, `â1` on odd), `hhafNeel_isHiddenAFM` (it is hidden-AFM on an even ring â all sites are `Âą`, every `IsNextPM` pair is cyclically adjacent hence opposite parity, via `inCyclicOpen_succ_mem_of_ne`), `hhafNeel_pmCount` (`Âą`-count `= L`), and `hhafNeelConfig0` (the NÃĐel configuration as a balanced charge-`0` configuration). **Ground energy âĪ âL PROVEN**: `hhafNeel_diag` (the NÃĐel diagonal energy is `âL`, every nearest-neighbour bond antiferromagnetic `(+1)(â1) = â1`) and `hhafMinEnergy_le_neg_L` (the global restricted ground energy `hhafMinEnergy âĪ âL`, via the Rayleigh quotient `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag` at the NÃĐel state) â the first half of the inter-sector ordering (the balanced sector reaches energy `âL`; the remaining half bounds the magnetization-`Âą1` single-`Âą` sectors above this). Toward that bound: `hhaf_diag_eq_zero_of_pmCount_one` â a single-`Âą` configuration has **vanishing diagonal energy** (every nearest-neighbour bond has a `0`-spin endpoint since only one site is `Âą`), the Gershgorin-disc centre for the magnetization-`Âą1` sectors. `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_raising_lowering` / `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_lowering_raising` â the spin-`1` ladder off-diagonal amplitude `|âĻÏ'|Å_xÂ·Å_y|ÏâĐ| âĪ 1` (each `â` factor `âĪ â2` for `N = 2`), the Gershgorin-disc radius ingredient. `hhafDressedMatrix_abs_le_ringCouplingSym` â for two hidden-AFM configs related by a single ladder move on a bond `{x, y}`, the dressed off-diagonal entry satisfies `|M Ï Ï| âĪ (ringCouplingSym x y).re` (the Marshall signs are `Âą1`, the Heisenberg element is `ringCouplingSym Â· spinSDot`, and the ladder amplitude is `âĪ 1`). **Magnetization-`Âą1` sector min `âĨ â2` PROVEN** (codex insight: the cyclic `IsHiddenAFMConfig` forbids odd `pmCount > 1`, so the only HAF sectors are balanced `Sz=0` (even `pmCount`) and single-`Âą` `Sz=Âą1` (`pmCount = 1`) â there are **no higher-`Sz` sectors**, dissolving the inter-sector LiebâMattis wall): `hhafConfigPM1` (the `pmCount = 1` subtype) with `hhafDressedMatrixPM1` (dressed block), `hhafPM1Site` (the unique `Âą` site, computed via `Finset.min'` so `Finset.image` reductions stay light), `hhafPM1_coupling_imp` (a nonzero off-diagonal forces `Ï` to be the slide `slidePM` of `Ï`'s `Âą` spin to its new site, sharing the `Âą` value by magnetization conservation `magEigenvalueS_eq_iff`), `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_offdiag_le` (per-entry bound `âĪ ringCouplingSym`), `hhafPM1Site_injOn_support` (the `Âą`-site map is injective on the coupling support), `ringCouplingSym_re_row_sum` (`â_t (ringCouplingSym s t).re = 2`, the two incident directed ring bonds), the generic `sum_comp_le_sum_univ_of_injOn` (injective-on-support reindex avoiding a heavy concrete `Finset.univ`), and `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_rowSum_le_two` âđ `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` (Gershgorin `eigenvalue_mem_ball`, diagonal `0` + row sum `âĪ 2`: every single-`Âą` eigenvalue `âĨ â2`) â the single-kink tight-binding lower bound placing the `Sz = Âą1` sectors above the balanced ground energy (`âĪ âL âĪ â2`). **Balanced ground energy `< âL` STRICT PROVEN** (`hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_lt_neg_L`): the PerronâFrobenius lowest eigenvector `v > 0` paired with the NÃĐel row gives `ÎžÂ·v(NÃĐel) = âLÂ·v(NÃĐel) + ÎĢ_{Ïâ NÃĐel} Mâ(NÃĐel,Ï) v(Ï)`; every off-diagonal term is `âĪ 0` (`hhafDressedMatrix0_offdiag_nonpos`) and the annihilation neighbour (`hhafNeel_step_neighbor`, annihilating the adjacent opposite-`Âą` pair at sites `0,1`) is strictly `< 0`, so via the generic row inequality `row_sum_mul_lt_diag_mul_of_offdiag_nonpos_exists_neg` and `hhafDressedMatrix0_Neel_diag` (`Mâ(NÃĐel,NÃĐel) = âL`) we get `ÎžÂ·v(NÃĐel) < âLÂ·v(NÃĐel)`, hence `Îž < âL`. Since `âL âĪ â2`, the balanced sector lies **strictly** below the magnetization-`Âą1` sectors with **no `L=2` tie** â the inter-sector ordering is complete. **Sector classification PROVEN** (`hhaf_pmCount_eq_one_or_even`): every hidden-AFM configuration has `pmCount = 1` or even `pmCount` â an odd count `âĨ 3` is impossible. Proof: the `Âą` sites read in increasing index order are pairwise `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_consecutive` â no `Âą` spin lies strictly between index-adjacent `Âą` sites, via `orderEmbOfFin`), so their signs strictly alternate (`hhaf_pm_alternates`: the `i`-th `Âą` value is the `0`-th when `i` is even and its complement `2 - Â·` when odd, `pm_flip`); and the maximal/minimal `Âą` sites are also `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_wrap` â the cyclic wrap arc holds no `Âą` spin), which would force the first and last `Âą` signs to *differ* while the linear alternation makes them *equal* at odd count â contradiction. The magnetization corollaries `hhaf_magSumS_eq_L_of_even` (even `pmCount âđ magSumS = L`, via `magSumS`-invariance under HAF ladder moves `magSumS_eq_of_hhafReachable` + reachability from canonical) and `hhaf_magSumS_ne_L_of_pmCount_one` (single-`Âą` âđ `magSumS = L Âą 1 â  L`) identify the sectors: balanced `= Sz 0`, single-`Âą` `= Sz Âą1`, with no higher sectors â the cross-sector vanishing (magnetization conservation) needed to split a ground eigenvector into balanced + single-`Âą` blocks. **Magnetization-block structure of the restricted matrix PROVEN**: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` (entries between different-`magSumS` configurations vanish â magnetization conservation); the generic slice `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice P` (submatrix on `{Ï // P Ï}`) with `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice_mulVec_of_full_eigen` (a full eigenvector restricted to a block-closed `P`-slice is a `P`-slice eigenvector with the same eigenvalue); and the two block-closures `hhafRestrictedMatrix_pmCount_one_block_closed` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_even_block_closed` (via the classification: a single-`Âą` config couples only to single-`Âą`, an even config only to even) â the block-diagonal decomposition into the balanced and single-`Âą` blocks, the structural input for the ground-eigenspace `finrank âĪ 1`. **Per-block spectral bounds PROVEN**: the complex blocks are the real blocks cast to `â` (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eq_map` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eq_map`, entries real); `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eigenspace_finrank_le_one` â the **complex balanced block ground eigenspace is one-dimensional** (realâcomplex bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real` from the real balanced PerronâFrobenius `finrank âĪ 1`); and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` â the **complex single-`Âą` block has all eigenvalues `âĨ â2`** (an eigenvalue is real, its re/im part is a real-block eigenvector, transferred to a dressed-block eigenvector via the Marshall conjugation `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eq_conj` where the Gershgorin bound `âĨ â2` applies). These are the per-block spectral inputs: at the ground energy `E < â2` the single-`Âą` block is vacated and the balanced block contributes the unique ground state. **Reverse block restriction PROVEN**: `hhafSliceExtend P` (zero-extension of a `P`-slice vector to all of `hhafConfig`) with `hhafSliceExtend_ne_zero` and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_hhafSliceExtend` (if there is no coupling *into* a `P`-slice from its complement, the zero-extension of a `P`-slice eigenvector is a full eigenvector at the same eigenvalue) â the reverse counterpart of the forward slice restriction, used to lift the balanced PerronâFrobenius eigenvector to a full restricted-matrix eigenvector (so the balanced ground energy is in the restricted spectrum). (The PerronâFrobenius uniqueness development â kink moves through the Marshall transfer, the magnetization-`Âą1` Gershgorin bound, and the strict balanced ground energy â lives in the companion `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean`, with its combinatorial kink-move/balanced-sector core split off into `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` for build speed.) | `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrder.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` |

(8.2.1)â(8.2.4)): the two-parameter Haldane-phase model. `lambdaDChainHamiltonianS L Îŧ D = ÎĢ_{x,y} [openAnisotropicChainCoupling] Â· spinSDotXXZ x y Îŧ 2 + D ÎĢ_x (Å_x^{(3)})Âē` (eq. 8.2.1, `ÄĪ_{Îŧ,D}` = open XXZ chain with Ising anisotropy `Îŧ` + crystal field `D`; `Îŧ=1` â open-boundary version of the anisotropic model 8.1.1, agreeing in the bulk). `neelOrderParameterS`/`stringOrderParameterS` (markers, `ââââFin 3ââ`) are the thermodynamic double-limit NÃĐel (8.2.2) and den NijsâRommelse string (8.2.3) order parameters of the GS. `tasaki_theorem_8_3` (**AXIOM**, eq. 8.2.4): for `ÎŧâĨ0` and any `D`, `O_NÃĐel^{(Îą)} âĪ O_string^{(Îą)}` for all `Îą` â nonzero NÃĐel order (Ising AF phase) forces nonzero hidden order. `tasaki_neel_transverse_eq_nonneg` (`ÎŧâĨ0`): `O_NÃĐel^{(1)} = O_NÃĐel^{(2)} âĨ 0`. Proof: path-integral representation (Â§6.3) | `Quantum/SpinS/LambdaDModel.lean` |

(8.2.5)â(8.2.7)): the nonlocal unitary realizing hidden ZâÃZâ symmetry breaking. `ktUnitaryS L` (marker) is the KennedyâTasaki unitary `Ã_KT = â_{u<v} exp(iÏ Å_u^{(3)} Å_v^{(1)})` (eq. 8.2.5), with `ktUnitaryS_sq` (`Ã_KTÂē=1`) and `ktUnitaryS_selfAdjoint` (`Ã_KT=Ã_KTâ `) â a self-adjoint involution. `piRotationS L Îą = â_x exp(iÏ Å_x^{(Îą)})` (**concrete**, on-site matrix exponentials) is the Ï-rotation about axis `Îą`; `IsZ2Z2Invariant H` = `(Ã_Ï^{(Îą)})â  H Ã_Ï^{(Îą)} = H` for all `Îą` (commutes with all three Ï-rotations). `HasShortRangeInteraction`/`HasSomeShortRangeInteraction` (markers) capture range-`r` locality. `tasaki_prop_8_4` (**AXIOM**): for a short-range open-chain `ÄĪ`, `Ã_KT ÄĪ Ã_KT` is again short-range **iff** `ÄĪ` is ZâÃZâ invariant â the hidden-symmetry-breaking picture is effective exactly when `ÄĪ` has ZâÃZâ symmetry | `Quantum/SpinS/KennedyTasakiTransformation.lean` |
| `guWenHamiltonianS` / `IsSPTPhase` / `tasaki_oshikawa_8_3_3` | **Â§8.3.1âÂ§8.3.2 Symmetry protected topological (SPT) phase** (GuâWen / PollmannâTurnerâBergâOshikawa; eqs. (8.3.1)â(8.3.4)): the Haldane phase as an SPT phase. `guWenHamiltonianS L D B = anisotropicChainHamiltonianS L D + B ÎĢ_x Å_x^{(1)}` (eq. 8.3.4, S=1 chain + magnetic field B in 1-dir, breaks ZâÃZââZâ; B=0 â anisotropic 8.1.1). `tasaki_oshikawa_8_3_3` (**AXIOM**, eq. 8.3.3): the spin-`S` VBS string order `O_string^{(Îą)}` is `>0` for odd `S`, `=0` for even `S` (even/odd-`S` distinction); `tasaki_vbs_edge_degeneracy` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` AKLT open chain has `(S+1)Âē`-fold edge degeneracy. **SPT definition** (`def : Prop`, NOT axiom): `HamiltonianPath` (continuous `ââ` Hamiltonian, each short-range gapped-unique via `IsShortRangeGappedUniqueGS` marker); `ContinuouslyConnected`/`SymmetryConnected sym` (path with all `ÄĪ_s` having the symmetry `sym`); `IsTrivialPhase sym H` = symmetry-connected to a product state (`IsProductStateHamiltonian`); `IsSPTPhase sym H` = short-range gapped-unique + `sym H` + ÂŽtrivial. The Haldane phase is the prototypical SPT phase protected by ZâÃZâ (`IsZ2Z2Invariant`) | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTPhase.lean` |
| `IsTimeReversalInvariant` / `IsBondInversionInvariant` / `vbsInversionParityS` / `tasaki_spt_classification` | **Â§8.3.2âÂ§8.3.3 Protecting symmetries + topological indices for SPT** (PollmannâTurnerâBergâOshikawa; eqs. (8.3.6)â(8.3.10)): the Haldane phase is protected by any of three symmetries â (S1) ZâÃZâ (`IsZ2Z2Invariant`), (S2) time-reversal (`IsTimeReversalInvariant` marker), (S3) bond-centered inversion (`IsBondInversionInvariant` marker). `vbsInversionParityS L S` (marker, âĪ) + `tasaki_vbs_inversion_parity` (**AXIOM**): `Ã_inv|ÎĶ_VBS^SâĐ = (â1)^{LÂ·S}|ÎĶ_VBS^SâĐ` â odd `LÂ·S` âđ odd parity âđ Zâ obstruction to connecting to the trivial state. `IsSpinSVBSNontrivialSPT S` (marker) + `tasaki_spt_classification` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` VBS is a nontrivial SPT phase **iff `S` is odd** (even `S` â trivial). `entanglementEntropyS` (marker, eqs. 8.3.7â8.3.8): the bipartite entanglement entropy `âÎĢ p_j log p_j` from the Schmidt decomposition. Â§8.3.3 is heuristic; precise indices come in Â§8.3.4 (MPS) / Â§8.3.6 (Ogata) | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTTopologicalIndex.lean` |
| `IsTrivialProjectiveRep` / `tasaki_theorem_8_7` / `tasaki_corollary_8_5` | **Â§8.3.4 Matrix-product SPT index** (Theorem 8.7 Tachikawa + Corollary 8.5; eqs. (8.3.42)â(8.3.47)): the precise MPS invariant. A protecting symmetry `G` acts on the bond space by a projective representation with phase function (2-cocycle) `Ï : GâGââ` (`IsProjectiveRep` marker); it is **trivial** (`IsTrivialProjectiveRep` marker) iff `Ï` is a coboundary (eq. 8.3.43) â the cohomology class is the SPT index. `SymmetricInjectiveMPSExists G Ï` (marker): an injective MPS invariant up to phase under `VĖ(g)`. `tasaki_theorem_8_7` (**AXIOM**): symmetric injective MPS âđ trivial projective rep. For half-odd-integer spin (`N` odd), `z2z2Spin_nontrivial_of_odd` (**AXIOM**, eq. 2.1.31): the ZâÃZâ rep is nontrivial. `tasaki_corollary_8_5` (**PROVED**, contrapositive of Thm 8.7): for `N` odd there is **no** ZâÃZâ-invariant injective MPS â the matrix-product LiebâSchultzâMattis no-go | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTMatrixProductIndex.lean` |
| `tasaki_theorem_8_6` | **Â§8.3.5 Discrete-symmetry LiebâSchultzâMattis theorem** (Theorem 8.6, OgataâTasaki): the general no-go without continuous symmetry. For **half-odd-integer** spin (`S=N/2`, `N` odd) with a short-ranged translation-invariant Hamiltonian (`HasShortRangeHamiltonianS`/`IsTranslationInvariantS` markers) having **either** ZâÃZâ **or** time-reversal symmetry (`IsZ2Z2SymmetricS âĻ IsTimeReversalSymmetricS`), the ground state can **never** be both unique and gapped: `tasaki_theorem_8_6` (**AXIOM**) `âđ ÂŽHasUniqueGappedGroundStateS H`. Unlike the original LSM (Â§6.2, continuous U(1)), this needs only a discrete symmetry; proved rigorously by OgataâTasaki via the Cuntz algebra / split property | `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisDiscrete.lean` |
| `sptInterpolatingHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_8` | **Â§8.3.6 Rigorous SPT phase transition** (Theorem 8.8, Tasaki 2018 index theorem; eq. (8.3.55)): the existence of a topological phase transition. `sptInterpolatingHamiltonianS L s = sÂ·akltHamiltonianS L + (1âs)Â·ÎĢ_x (Å_x^{(3)})Âē` (eq. 8.3.55) linearly connects the trivial Hamiltonian (8.1.2, `s=0`, large-`D` phase) to the AKLT/VBS Hamiltonian (7.1.1, `s=1`, Haldane phase), keeping all three protecting symmetries. `tasaki_theorem_8_8` (**AXIOM**): the model's infinite-volume limit undergoes a phase transition â â intermediate `sâ(0,1)` at which the family `LâĶÄĪ_s` is **either** gapless (`IsGaplessInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has multiple ground states (`HasMultipleGroundStatesInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has a local-expectation discontinuity (`HasLocalExpectationDiscontinuityInInfiniteVolume`) â markers on the `Lââ` family. Proving topological phase transitions resists standard methods; Tasaki settled it via an index theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTPhaseTransition.lean` |
| `toricCodeHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_9` | **Â§8.4 Topological order: Kitaev's toric code** (Theorem 8.9, BravyiâHastingsâMichalakis; eqs. (8.4.1), (8.4.19)): a gapped phase whose degeneracy is protected by topology, not symmetry. Qubits live on the edges `ToricEdge L = (ZMod L Ã ZMod L) Ã Fin 2` of an `LÃL` torus (`2LÂē` edges); `starEdges`/`plaquetteEdges` are the 4-edge stars `A_v`/plaquettes `B_p`; `sigmaZS`/`sigmaXS` = `Ï^z`/`Ï^x`. `toricCodeHamiltonianS L = âÎĢ_v â_{A_v} Ï^z â ÎĢ_p â_{B_p} Ï^x` (eq. 8.4.1, commuting stabilizers, gap âĨ 2, 4-fold degenerate on the torus). `perturbedToricHamiltonianS L Îĩ V = ÄĪ_tc + Îĩ ÎĢ_x V_x` (eq. 8.4.19). `tasaki_theorem_8_9` (**AXIOM**): for range-`r` perturbations there are **L-independent** `Îĩâ,Î>0` such that for `|Îĩ|âĪÎĩâ`, all `LâĨ2` (nontrivial torus), and any local perturbation (`IsToricPerturbation`), the perturbed Hamiltonian has **four near-degenerate ground states** separated by `Î` (`HasFourNearlyDegenerateGroundStates`) â topological 4-fold degeneracy stable under arbitrary perturbation, no symmetry needed. Lattice + stabilizers + both Hamiltonians concrete | `Quantum/SpinS/ToricCode.lean` |
